anchor: ...see United States v. Wright-Barker, 784 F.2d 161, 176 (3d Cir.1986), superseded by statute on other grounds as stated in United States v. Edmond, 431 U.S. 606, 615 n.8 (1977)...
positive: the Coast Guard may conduct warrantless searches of American vessels
hard_negatives: the Coast Guard lacks jurisdiction over foreign vessels
warrants are required for all maritime inspections
reasonable suspicion is never required for searches
the Fourth Amendment applies differently at sea
full_context: ...see United States v. Wright-Barker, 784 F.2d 161, 176 (3d Cir.1986) (the Coast Guard may conduct warrantless searches of American vessels), superseded by statute on other grounds as stated in United States v. Edmond, 431 U.S. 606, 615 n.8 (1977)...
